item.title: Privacy-Enhancing Machine Learning
item.description:
Hi!

I wanted to share my MSc thesis Privacy-Enhancing Machine Learning (2024), based mostly on my first year working at GMV.

My tutors were Carlos Gregorio Rodríguez and Juan Miguel Auñón García (if you have the opportunity, I recommend working with them, I learnt a lot).

Here is the PDF :)

item.title: Aproximación de puntos vía curvas de Bézier
item.description:
Hi!

I wanted to share my BSc thesis from 2023. I don’t think anyone will find much value in it, I just wanted to mix design and math and play around with it 🙂

I’ll keep it here, in my own little corner of the internet, as a memory of a time I hold close to my heart. A lot happened that year. I started living with my boyfriend in a tiny apartment in Madrid, studying A LOT, and working part-time to cover expenses. It was exhausting, but I miss it somehow.

item.title: PCA is equivalent to a Linear Autoencoder
item.description:
Our objective is to compact the representation of data while minimizing the information loss using linear transformations.

Principal Component Analysis (PCA)

Given $n$ data vector $\{x_1, \dots, x_n\} \in \mathbb{R}^m$, assuming that are mean centered, and given a target number of dimensions $k < m$. PCA finds an orthonormal basis $\{u_1, \dots, u_k\} \in \mathbb{R}^m$ that explains most of the variation of the projected data. Let $U \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times k}$ the matrix whose columns are $u_j \in \mathbb{R}^m$ and $X \in \mathbb{R}^{m \times n}$ is a matrix whose columns are the data vector $x_j \in \mathbb{R}^m$.

item.title: How to create a simple Python project
item.description:
In this post I’d like to write down the steps to make the simplest Python project you could make. Mainly focused for research projects (e.g. people in academia). Therefore, there will be no pipelines (like Github Actions or Jenkins), nor any docs.

Version control

Let’s say you have created your local directory: mkdir my-project. First, you need to init the version control. As of today, the most used tool is git.
